Our experience has been quite the ride. My husband was laid off/ terminated due to a work injury approx. a year and a half ago. We heard good things about Campos law group and decided to give them a call. At first everything was good. Minimal communication, but that was expected as we know it's a long process. However, we are in the final stretch, and the communication has only gotten worse. In the beginning we were being assisted by a paralegal named Ashley/ Ashlynn?, something along those lines. We had direct contact with her and she would always get back in a timely manner. But now, the person who has taken over his case is named Brenda. She's nice and professional (when he can get her on the phone), but never has any answers. it feels like she's just stringing him along. Its always, "I will call you back", "let me check with the lawyer", "I don't have that info at the moment"... BUT, getting ahold of her is INSANELY HARD!!! She's never in the office or always on another call.

I know they don't have the same urgency as we do to resolve this case. But they should have the clients well-being as their priority. My husband was told not to work while his case was active, his disability benefits have finished, and they say that he isn't eligible for workers comp. He hasn't had any income in a couple months.

Yet there is no resolution in sight. Or at least not that we know of because we haven't been able to get ahold of someone who can give him info on his case. We have considered driving over there, but we are quite a distance away. Everything has been done via phone and email. I don't want to take a day, and it be wasted because they aren't in the office that day.

They really need to work on their communication skills, and actually getting back to people when they say. Otherwise don't commit to call backs or to having information by certain dates. Its really frustrating that as a professional business (especially a law firm) they do not keep their word.

What made this experience even more unacceptable is that throughout the entire process, I never once spoke directly with the actual attorney handling my case. All communication went through staff, and even that was inconsistent and unhelpful. At no point did I feel properly represented, advised, or guided by a lawyer, which is exactly what you are trusting and paying a law firm for.

The overall lack of professionalism from this office was shocking. Instead of feeling supported during a stressful time, I felt like an inconvenience for simply asking questions about my own case. There was no transparency about timelines, no clear explanations, and no proactive communication at any point. I constantly felt left in the dark and had no confidence that my case was being handled with care or urgency.

The case dragged on for almost two years with very little progress unless I personally pushed for updates. This unnecessary delay caused a great deal of stress and frustration. A client should never have to chase their own legal representation just to ensure things are moving forward.

When the case was finally resolved, I was left feeling deeply uncomfortable and disappointed with the outcome. Given the amount of time, effort, and emotional energy I had to put into staying on top of everything, I genuinely felt that I was shorted on the funds I received. The final result did not reflect the length of time the case took or the lack of support and advocacy I experienced.

Overall, this law office failed to meet even the most basic standards of communication, professionalism, and client care. Instead of easing stress, they added to it significantly. I regret choosing this firm and would not recommend them to anyone looking for reliable, respectful, and attentive legal representation

reviews.ownerResponse:
Online reviews are always difficult for both the establishment where the review is being left and the consumer/customer who will read it. And they're difficult because unless the establishment responds, only the version of the person who left the review is visible.
Therefore, Ms. Moreno, I regret your version of the question you had with me, but it seems fair that I respond to your complaints point by point. The vast majority of my clients over the last 21 years come from far away and are not locals. They come from far away because of the recommendations of my satisfied customers... just like Mr. Martin Gómez, who recommended you. Based on his recommendation, you decided to drive your 45 minutes to see me.
Regarding your second point, about your 30-minute wait, you are right. It's not uncommon for people with an appointment to have to wait a bit. I charge for my consultations, but even so, I DON'T SET A TIME LIMIT, and I give every client the opportunity to explain their situation. There are consultations that take 10 minutes and others that take an hour. After so many years as a lawyer, I already know what questions to ask the client based on the information they give us and can decide whether or not I can help them.
When I consult with you, I shouldn't rush you either and listen to your whole story. I don't want to embarrass you, but since you say you "don't even listen to me," I feel I have to respond and explain that I HEAR EVERYTHING. I explained my opinion of the fight you had in which the police came out. I heard them tell you they were going to file criminal charges. And I heard much more about the entire incident. I EXPLAINED that, at that point, you STILL didn't need to pay for a lawyer to represent you. I explained that the document you showed me was written as if you were a victim. When she insisted on knowing what was going to happen, and I explained it to her again… you still didn't believe what I was saying. She asked me the same questions in different ways, hoping I'd give her a different answer. Even so, I offered to be available for calls in case the police called her or she received a letter.
Finally, the people who always leave only one star are the ones who can't be helped. I understand it's difficult to hear the truth or why they can't be helped. But I don't usually hear people hiring me when there's nothing I can do for them. I'm direct and to the point. If I can help you, I explain what we can do, how much it will cost, and how long it will take. If I can't help you, I also explain why. I'm sorry I didn't tell you what you wanted to hear, but again… THERE WAS NOTHING TO BE DONE AT THE MOMENT.

I wish you luck.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Rudy,
Immigration law is an everchanging animal. I cannot speak to your wife's case, but I can tell you that the evaluation that was provided at the time was correct. I have had many cases in which new court decisions impact past evaluations and cases in which other honest attorneys have said no, I have said yes. There are many cases in which clients come in and had they sought legal advice earlier, their cases would have been resolved. When it comes to immigration law, evaluations are valid only for the day they are given. The next day, a court can issue a new decision that makes yesterday's evaluation obsolete. Yes, don't ever give up. Always, seek legal advice as things may change tomorrow.
